examples.title: Two's Complement Conversion
examples.description: Convert decimal to n-bit two's complement and vice versa
examples.explanation: to_twos_complement converts negative numbers by adding 2^bits. from_twos_complement checks MSB and applies sign extension to recover original value.

examples.title: Overflow Detection in Signed Addition
examples.description: C program to detect overflow in two's complement addition
examples.explanation: Overflow detection logic: if both operands have same sign but result has different sign, overflow occurred. The C program demonstrates this with 3 test cases showing overflow and non-overflow scenarios.

examples.title: Binary/Decimal/Hex/Octal Converter
examples.description: Convert integers between different number systems
examples.explanation: This converter first converts input to decimal, then from decimal to the target base. Python's built-in bin(), oct(), and hex() functions are used, with string manipulation to clean the output.

examples.title: Sign Extension for Two's Complement
examples.description: Java program demonstrating sign extension for signed integers
examples.explanation: Sign extension works by replicating the sign bit to the left. For positive numbers, add 0s; for negative two's complement, add 1s. This preserves the original value when increasing bit width.
